steps.title: Find the side length
steps.content:
An equilateral triangle has three equal sides. Since the perimeter is $894$ centimeters, divide the perimeter by $3$ to get one side length:
$$
\frac{894}{3}=298
$$
So each side of the equilateral triangle is $298$ centimeters.

steps.title: Use the circumradius formula
steps.content: For an equilateral triangle, the radius of the circumscribed circle is the side length multiplied by $\sqrt{3}$ and divided by $3$. This comes from the fact that the circumcenter lies two-thirds of the way along a median.

steps.title: Solve for the coefficient
steps.content: Substitute $s=298$ into the formula: $$ R=\frac{298\sqrt{3}}{3} $$ The problem writes the radius as $w\sqrt{3}$, so the coefficient of $\sqrt{3}$ is the value of $w$: $$ w=\frac{298}{3} $$ The answer is 298/3.
